name: Noctcom
description: Noctcom is an open-source, zero-knowledge encrypted file storage platform. All files are encrypted in the browser before upload — the server only ever stores ciphertext, and the encryption password never leaves your device. Self-hosting is free forever (AGPL-3.0). The stack runs on Docker Compose: PostgreSQL, Redis, MinIO (S3-compatible blob storage), a Fastify API, a Next.js frontend and Caddy.

notes.text: Zero-knowledge by design: the encryption password is NOT recoverable. If a user loses it, their files cannot be decrypted by anyone.

The container is installed in LAN mode (HTTP, no TLS): app on http://<IP>, API on http://<IP>:3000. Reserve the container's IP in your DHCP server — the API URL is baked into the frontend build. If the IP changes, edit the URLs in /opt/noctcom/.env and run 'docker compose up -d --build'.

To serve it on a real domain with automatic TLS (Let's Encrypt via Caddy), edit /opt/noctcom/.env (CADDY_DOMAIN, PUBLIC_URL, PUBLIC_API_URL, FRONTEND_URL, COMPOSE_FILE=docker-compose.yml) and run 'docker compose up -d --build'.
Email verification/OTP is disabled by default. Add RESEND_API_KEY or SMTP_* settings to /opt/noctcom/.env to enable it.
